concept_decisions.labels_compared: surveillance_measurement_designs vs ascertainment_correction_hidden_burden; surveillance_system_reporting_interventions; environmental_digital_surveillance; diagnostic_case_definition_validity; spatial_risk_mapping_detection_adjusted; reporting_adjusted_dynamics_forecasting; counterfactual_policy_vaccination_impact; surveillance_gap_evidence_synthesis; misascertainment_bias_simulation_methods
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: Manifest, named concept table, and titles plus complete available abstracts for all 150 assigned records.
concept_decisions.rationale: The provisional bucket mixes distinct method designs: empirical ascertainment correction, surveillance-system interventions, environmental/digital surveillance, diagnostic-validity studies, spatial risk mapping, reporting-adjusted epidemic models, policy counterfactuals, evidence syntheses, and general misascertainment methodology. Keeping a single residual surveillance-design concept would remain semantically vague.

reassignments.rationale: Perspective on missing gender-identity reporting in COVID-19 trials; core design is guidance on reporting gaps.
Empirical review of trial result underreporting and discontinuation; central contribution is evidence on reporting gaps in the research record.
Systematic review of emergency vaccine trial protocols and reporting exclusions.
Population serocohort quantifying infections missed by routine case surveillance.
Wastewater-based surveillance using sewer measurements as complementary case-detection data.
Operationalizes wastewater metrics as surveillance indicators for community COVID activity.
Method perspective centered on integrating wastewater data with dynamic epidemic models.
Bayesian epidemic forecasting model explicitly incorporating underreported cases and reporting delays.
Validates EHR code-based surveillance definitions against chart review gold standards.
Bayesian correction of gestational syphilis under-registration to estimate true incidence.
Wastewater epidemiology for MDR-TB surveillance outside routine clinical reporting.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance system design and reporting interventions
reassignments.rationale: Implements and evaluates a real-time monitoring and alerting system to reduce missed infectious disease reports.
Scoping review of digital surveillance tools for post-discharge surgical-site infection detection.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance system design and reporting interventions
reassignments.rationale: Compares passive and active surveillance components to quantify outbreak-detection contributions.

new_concepts.rationale: Empirical studies that estimate true incidence, mortality, or reporting completeness from serology, linkage, excess deaths, standardized corrections, or other surveillance-calibration data.

new_concepts.canonical_name: Surveillance system design and reporting interventions
new_concepts.rationale: Studies that design, implement, compare, or evaluate reporting workflows, active surveillance, notification systems, signal-detection systems, or case-finding interventions.
Studies using wastewater, web search, vector/environmental streams, or other nonclinical data sources as complementary surveillance channels.
Studies validating diagnostic algorithms, case definitions, proxy measures, reporting agreement, or registry completeness.

new_concepts.canonical_name: Spatial risk mapping with detection/reporting adjustment
new_concepts.rationale: Geospatial or ecological niche models that locate hotspots or risk areas while explicitly addressing imperfect detection or reporting.
Transmission, incidence, or forecasting models that estimate reporting rates or otherwise structurally account for under-ascertainment in epidemic dynamics.

new_concepts.canonical_name: Counterfactual policy and vaccination impact models
new_concepts.rationale: Counterfactual or intervention-effect models estimating policy or vaccination impacts under incomplete observed reporting.
Systematic reviews, scoping reviews, narrative reviews, expert reports, or guidance documents centered on surveillance, reporting, or diagnostic gaps.
General statistical, simulation, or methodological papers focused on misascertainment bias itself rather than a single surveillance application.
